header_summary:
Junhyuk Jun Lee's inquiry into the long-term economic implications of Bitcoin's decreasing block reward, particularly around the year 2040, underscores the importance of predictive analysis for future mining dynamics and network security. This discussion forms part of a broader dialogue within the Bitcoin Development Mailing List, which focuses on fostering a robust framework to support Bitcoin's growth despite diminishing miner incentives. [More information on this discussion](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CAKaEYh+t_sPhQaNL91_Pmwc9HdVqQjzXKMDUy_YpsaLCrJ1zhw@mail.gmail.com/T/#m493c8674a4abcad7b1806940c90671b6edd4254b).

The release of Bitcoin Core 31.1 introduces significant enhancements, including bug fixes, performance improvements, and security updates, aimed at maintaining the software's reliability for diverse operating systems. Users can access this update and contribute to its ongoing refinement through various channels provided on the project’s website. [Explore the new version of Bitcoin Core](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-31.1/).

Conversations around the design of a cost accounting system for blockchain transactions highlight the necessity of preparing for quantum computing impacts and balancing decentralization with increased transaction capacity. This dialogue emphasizes the critical nature of adaptable blockchain protocol designs that can handle future technological shifts without disruptive updates, ensuring network operability and security. [Further details are available here](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/segwit-commitment-to-post-quantum-witness-data/2702/6).

Lastly, the discussion on enhancing the searchability of Bitcoin-specific content through advanced machine learning models, like those employed in the BitcoinSearch App, reflects a growing need to better navigate and utilize extensive Bitcoin resources online. This project seeks to close gaps in content coverage and improve the comprehensiveness of search tools, enhancing access to valuable Bitcoin-related information. [Learn more about this initiative](https://github.com/bitcoinsearch/bitcoinsearch-app).

recent_posts.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards: preventing miner deviation as the block rewards approaches zero
recent_posts.summary:
- Junhyuk Jun Lee inquired about Bitcoin's subsidy reductions and its 2040 implications.
- His question underlines concerns over long-term economic impacts and Bitcoin viability.
- These discussions contribute to strategic planning within the Bitcoin community.

recent_posts.title: The game-theory problems of PQ sunsetting modes
recent_posts.summary: - The exploration of game-theory and quantum computing impacts Bitcoin's security and economics.  - Collusion between miners and quantum entities could enable large-scale coin extractions.  - Further research is essential to counter quantum threats to blockchain integrity.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v31.1 Released
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version 31.1 is released, featuring bug fixes and performance enhancements.
- Upgrade requires full application shutdown, with installation steps varying by OS.
- The update includes significant technical enhancements and extensive contributor acknowledgment.

recent_posts.title: Segwit commitment to post-quantum witness data?
recent_posts.summary:
- The email examines the design of a complex cost accounting system for blockchain transactions.
- It discusses preparing for increased transaction demand due to quantum computing advancements.
- Importance of adaptable blockchain protocol design to avoid disruptive updates is emphasized.

recent_posts.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions
recent_posts.summary: - Specialized Bitcoin content lacks in traditional search engines.  - BitcoinSearch App aims to index diverse Bitcoin-focused information.  - Integration with model-based platforms could enhance content retrieval and utility.

recent_posts.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards : preventing miner's deviation when the Bitcoin rewards is zero
recent_posts.summary:
- The paper's title needs modification to accurately reflect its scope and findings.
- It is crucial to clarify that the assumptions represent a pessimistic scenario.
- Addressing critiques thoroughly will enhance the study's academic robustness.

active_posts.title: DahLIAS: Discrete Logarithm-Based Interactive Aggregate Signatures
active_posts.summary:
- DahLIAS introduces a new aggregate signature scheme for more efficient Bitcoin transactions.
- The scheme allows faster verification and operational efficiency while avoiding key tweaking vulnerabilities.
- It is a notable advancement in cryptographic protocols, emphasizing secure and space-efficient transactions.

active_posts.title: BIP draft: Full-Aggregation of BIP 340 Signatures
active_posts.summary:
- A new Bitcoin Improvement Proposal suggests full-aggregation of BIP 340 signatures.
- Developers have proposed using Cross-Input Signature Aggregation to reduce Bitcoin transaction sizes.
- Stakeholders are invited to review and comment on the proposal via GitHub.

active_posts.title: Quantum Recovery Of Hashed Address Secured Coins With No Confiscatory Risk
active_posts.summary:
- Quantum-safe signature schemes are essential for cryptocurrency security in quantum breakthroughs.
- Various proposals address ECC-based coin security, but none offer a universal solution.
- Layered recovery approaches could potentially cover all key types except non-hashed addresses.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
active_posts.summary: - SegData introduces efficient, optional-data storage in Bitcoin via a soft-fork.  - It allows data pruning, with costs borne by operators choosing to retain data.  - Potential adopters include indexers and timestamping services, but not data requiring script evaluation.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal aims to optimize BIP-360 by recovering public keys from EC signatures.
- This recovery reduces witness size and avoids increasing quantum exposure in blockchain transactions.
- Modifications in witness validation and nonce derivation are suggested to ensure security and efficiency.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary: - Bitcoin privacy discussions focus on sidechains, L2s, and wallet integrations.  - Layer 1 privacy is less emphasized due to regulatory and technical challenges.  - Current enhancements prioritize regulatory compatibility and integration ease.

today_in_history_posts.title: On the experiment of the Bitcoin Contracting Primitives WG and marking this community process "up for grabs"
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- A new community process for Bitcoin covenant specification was initiated following various discussions and trial failures.
- The Bitcoin Contracting Primitives Working Group was formed to document and archive advanced contract-related technologies.
- Despite early promise, the author shifted focus from the covenant process to researching CoinPool and Lightning Network development.

today_in_history_posts.title: Blinded Paths Doom Scenario
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Blinded paths in the Lightning Network protect receiver privacy but limit routing options.
- Compliant nodes may dominate, threatening decentralization and enhancing privacy risks.
- Chain analysis concerns are raised, highlighting pressures on businesses and potential privacy trade-offs.

today_in_history_posts.title: Chain Code Delegation: Private Access Control for Bitcoin Keys
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Jesse Posner developed Chain Code Delegation to enhance custody privacy and security.
- This method limits custodial access by using scalar tweaks instead of full chain codes.
- Chain Code Delegation reduces risks by confining damage to specific compromised transactions.
